dimensions: No raw numbers or specific targets
Required analytics events listed with Requirement IDs
Required test scenarios cover happy path, edge cases, and error paths
Performance thresholds defined qualitatively with measurement methodology
Machine-checkable acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format
Every user story maps to at least one acceptance criterion
Requirement IDs assigned to all discrete requirements

hard_fail_checks: Architecture decisions present in Part 1 (violates Rule #2)
Specific numerical targets without measurement methodology (violates Rule #1)
Component-based organization instead of user-story format (violates Rule #3)
Missing Requirement IDs on discrete requirements

vague_language.action: Flag for specificity — each instance must be accompanied by measurable criteria

tautology.description: Circular definitions where the requirement restates the solution

missing_acceptance_criteria.description: User story without Given/When/Then or equivalent

hedging_language.action: Flag — PRDs state requirements, not hopes
